creator: adrian@freebsd.org
text:
ok, I have ixbge mdio working after a bunch of hilarity.

However, even with the diff above, I'm still having issues with the driver connecting.

Here's an MDIO trace including both the ixgbe MDIO IO and the single phy read when the driver tries to bring up the first switchport phy. The register reading 0xffff makes me think the PHY isn't powered on?

mdio0: <MDIO> on ix0
mdioproxy0: <MII/MDIO proxy, MDIO side> on mdio0
mdio1: <MDIO> on ix1
mdioproxy1: <MII/MDIO proxy, MDIO side> on mdio1
ix0: ixgbe_mdio_readreg: called (phy 16 / reg 0x0000) = data 0x1b99, ret=0
ix0: ixgbe_mdio_writereg: called (phy 16 / reg 0x0000 = data 0x9803), ret=0
ix0: ixgbe_mdio_readreg: called (phy 16 / reg 0x0000) = data 0x1803, ret=0

ix0: ixgbe_mdio_readreg: called (phy 16 / reg 0x0001) = data 0xffff, ret=0
e6000sw0: e6000sw_readphy_locked: (phy=0000 reg=0001) = 0xffff
e6000sw0: failed to attach miibus (err 6)
device_attach: e6000sw0 attach returned 6


... I don't have access to the marvell switch and PHY datasheets so I'm not sure yet what's going on. Do you have any insights?

Thanks!
